mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate trend increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real sales of new houses indicate a synchronized housing market contraction that reduces construction activity and consumer leverage, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index above its 12-month moving average reflects robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive signal during early-cycle expansion yields negative 5-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the 200 index threshold while the federal funds rate remains above 5 percent, real borrowing costs become restrictive and corporate margins compress, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations meeting the threshold condition.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates healthy job creation without labor market slack, supporting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth combined with stable UNRATE fails to produce positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter growth rate in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a supply-side contraction that reduces business investment and inventory restocking, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
